summary:
The loss of an antagonist leads to increased width in the periodontal ligament space as seen through radiography; this results from decreased mechanical stress typically exerted by biting forces. \boxed{A}

The summary captures that removal of an antagonist causes expansion of the periodontal ligament area via absence of normal bite-related pressures, matching Agent2’s core point about altered spatial dimensions under changed biomechanical conditions without exceeding two concise statements.
